description: CORRECTNESS-TRAP fixture. Correct-but-unoptimized windowed sum: csim already PASSES. The supplied mock_patch is a genuine C++ semantic change (drops the last term of every window) that looks like a speed-up but produces WRONG output under g++ csim. The optimize loop MUST re-verify csim, detect the regression, discard the broken child, and keep the correct baseline. This is the safety-invariant credibility test.
expected: csim_pass=true at baseline; a throughput-improving but correctness-breaking patch is REJECTED and the baseline is preserved (improved=false)
